reasoning_model_1: Model 1 completely failed to generate the required spoken content. The audio consists entirely of a repetitive, 40-second loop of coughing sounds without any intelligible speech or adherence to the text script ('不好意思，我刚才说到哪里了...'). According to the global considerations, since the content is empty/missing, this model must receive a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 successfully synthesized the full text script with high accuracy and natural fluency. Regarding the specific paralinguistic requirement (speaking with coughing), the model interprets the text '咳咳' (Cough cough) at the beginning (00:00) somewhat literally. It produces a sound that resembles a quick, stiff throat-clearing or the phonetic pronunciation of 'Ke Ke' rather than a realistic, visceral non-verbal cough. The voice quality during the subsequent speech remains clear and does not reflect the physical strain or interruption associated with 'speaking while coughing'. While the specific feature performance is weak (more like reading onomatopoeia than simulating the act), the model fulfills the basic TTS task successfully.
model_comparison: Model 1 is a complete failure as it does not generate any speech content, only a loop of sound effects. Model 2 successfully delivers the content with natural prosody. Although Model 2's execution of the specific 'coughing' feature is subpar (sounding artificial/text-based rather than a natural non-verbal sound), it is the only model that followed the instruction to speak the text. Model 1 is unusable.
